solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public int strongPasswordChecker(String password) {
        int missing = 0;
        if (!password.contains("[a-z]")) missing++;
        if (!password.contains("[A-Z]")) missing++;
        if (!password.contains("[0-9]")) missing++;

        int replace = 0;
        int repeatCount = 0;
        for (int i = 2; i < password.length(); i++) {
            if (password.charAt(i) == password.charAt(i - 1) && password.charAt(i) == password.charAt(i - 2)) {
                repeatCount++;
                if (i == password.length() - 1 || password.charAt(i + 1) != password.charAt(i)) {
                    replace++;
                }
            }
        }

        int delete = 0;
        if (password.length() > 20) {
            delete = password.length() - 20;
            replace = Math.min(replace, delete);
        }

        int insert = 0;
        if (password.length() < 6) {
            insert = 6 - password.length();
        }

        int steps = Math.max(missing, replace + Math.max(0, insert - replace));
        steps += Math.max(0, delete - replace);
        return steps;
    }
}
```
efficiency: MEDIUM
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: <The solution efficiently checks for missing character types and repeating characters, then calculates the minimum steps required to meet the strong password criteria. It balances insertions, deletions, and replacements based on the constraints. The main weakness is the use of string contains for character types, which could be optimized with flags.>
